hooks.command: FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty'); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && [ -f "$FILE" ]; then EXT="${FILE##*.}"; DIR=$(dirname "$FILE"); BIOME=''; while [ "$DIR" != '/' ]; do if [ -f "$DIR/biome.json" ] || [ -f "$DIR/biome.jsonc" ]; then BIOME="$DIR"; break; fi; DIR=$(dirname "$DIR"); done; if [ -n "$BIOME" ]; then pnpm exec biome format --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; else case "$EXT" in js|jsx|ts|tsx|json|css|md) npx prettier --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null ;; py) black --quiet "$FILE" 2>/dev/null ;; esac; fi; fi; exit 0

COMMAND=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.command'); if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE 'rm\s+-rf\s+/|git\s+push.*--force|git\s+push.*-f'; then echo "Blocked: destructive command detected. Use trash or non-force alternatives." >&2; exit 2; else exit 0; fi

hooks.prompt: Review the conversation so far. Has the agent completed ALL acceptance criteria from the task? Did it run verification tests? Did it check for anti-Goodhart issues (tests validating behavior not just output)? If any of these are incomplete, respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "Incomplete: [what's missing]"}. If all done, respond with {"decision": "allow"}.

hooks.command: osascript -e 'display notification "Claude needs attention" with title "Claude Code"' 2>/dev/null || notify-send 'Claude Code' 'Claude needs attention' 2>/dev/null; exit 0
